overview: A modular orchestration layer for AI coding agents designed to provide long-term memory, code ownership, and multi-agent coordination within the Cursor ecosystem. It utilizes .mdc files for agent playbooks, a RALPH-style iterative loop for task execution, and an internal messaging system for agent collaboration.

requirements.title: Ownership Registry System
requirements.description: A centralized system to map specific code modules and directories to dedicated Agent Teams/Roles to ensure architectural consistency.
success_criteria: The system correctly resolves ownership using longest-prefix matching for any given file path.
CLI commands 'ace own', 'ace who', and 'ace list-owners' accurately reflect the state of .ace/ownership.yaml.
Each file is restricted to one primary owner while allowing secondary dependencies to be logged.

sub_requirements.title: Agent Registry
sub_requirements.description: Maintain a registry of all agents (manual or autonomous) in .ace/agents.yaml including metadata like ID, role, and memory file.
success_criteria: New agents can be created via 'ace agent create' and persist in the YAML registry.
Agent metadata includes unique email addresses for the Agent Mail system.

requirements.title: Dynamic Context Builder
requirements.description: A component that composes a specific 'context slice' for each agent call based on the task, the agent's role, and relevant project history.
success_criteria: Context composition includes global rules, agent-specific playbooks, recent ADRs, and session continuity data.
The 'ace context show' command outputs the exact string that would be prepended to a prompt.
Task-type framing (implement, review, debug, etc.) correctly modifies the prompt instructions.

requirements.title: Write-back Pipeline
requirements.description: An automated feedback loop where the orchestrator extracts lessons learned from an agent's output and updates the agent's .mdc playbook.
success_criteria: The reflection prompt successfully extracts new strategies [str-XXX] and pitfalls [mis-XXX] in JSON format.
The orchestrator performs incremental updates to .mdc files without overwriting existing manual content.
Helpful/Harmful counters are updated based on the success or failure of the task.

requirements.title: RALPH Loop Engine
requirements.description: An iterative execution motor (Reasoning, Action, Learning, Progress, Halt) that runs the agent until tests pass or limits are reached.
success_criteria: The 'ace loop' command successfully executes multiple iterations, refreshing context between each.
The engine halts immediately upon a successful test run (TDD approach).
The engine captures failure reasons in the learning phase to inform the next iteration.

sub_requirements.title: Headless Executor Integration
sub_requirements.description: Integration with cursor-agent or Claude Code CLI to execute tasks programmatically.
success_criteria: The system handles headless execution timeouts and retries gracefully.
Output is captured and streamed for real-time processing by the write-back pipeline.

requirements.title: Multi-Agent Coordination & Messaging
requirements.description: Systems for asynchronous communication and consensus-building between different specialized agents.
success_criteria: The Agent Mail system correctly stores and retrieves messages in .ace/mail/ folders.
Agents can 'subscribe' to changes in modules they depend on.
The Consensus Protocol triggers a debate when changes cross ownership boundaries.

sub_requirements.title: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
sub_requirements.description: Formal instructions for agent interactions such as onboarding, PR reviews, and audits.
success_criteria: The 'ace agent onboard' command generates a valid onboarding.md for new agents.
PR reviews are automatically triggered and logged when cross-module changes occur.

sub_requirements.title: Token Consumption Modes
sub_requirements.description: Global settings (Low, Medium, High) to control the depth of context and debate to manage costs.
success_criteria: Switching to 'Low' mode disables multi-agent debate and minimizes context slices.
Switching to 'High' mode enables proactive refactoring suggestions and deep context analysis.

requirements.title: UI/UX Orchestration (Google Stitch)
requirements.description: Integration with Google Stitch for AI-driven design mockups and code generation.
success_criteria: The 'ace ui mockup' command generates a design description based on project constraints.
The 'ace ui sync' command successfully imports Tailwind/Flutter code from a Stitch canvas URL.
Visual verification tests (Playwright) can validate the implemented UI against the mockup intent.

requirements.title: Global Project Memory (AGENTS.md)
requirements.description: A cross-tool project memory file located at the root to provide high-level context to any AI tool (Cursor, Claude, etc.).
success_criteria: AGENTS.md contains a current table of agent roles, responsibilities, and active ADRs.
The file includes a standardized 'Write-back Protocol' instruction for all agents to follow.

requirements.title: Quality Assurance & TDD Framework
requirements.description: A strict testing regime to ensure the stability of the orchestrator and the quality of agent-generated code.
success_criteria: 100% test pass rate on critical modules (Registry, Context Builder) in CI/CD.
Automated 'Agentic Experience' (AX) tests measure context relevance and write-back accuracy.
CLI ergonomics are validated to ensure low friction for developers (DX).
